introSection: Thunderhead is a wooden roller coaster located at Dollywood in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, USA. It was built by Great Coasters International and opened in 2004.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In June 2003, Thunderhead was announced.

Thunderhead opened on April 3, 2004.

In 2009 and 2018, portions of Thunderhead were retracked by Great Coasters International.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Thunderhead required 700,000 feet of Southern Yellow pine to construct the 3,230 foot long coaster.

It was the first roller coaster to feature a station fly through.

The ride has turns banked to 78 degrees, which was the steepest Great Coasters International had yet done when the ride opened.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 12 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 24 riders per train.

fullText:
Thunderhead is a wooden roller coaster located at Dollywood in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, USA. It was built by Great Coasters International and opened in 2004.

In June 2003, Thunderhead was announced.

Thunderhead opened on April 3, 2004.

In 2009 and 2018, portions of Thunderhead were retracked by Great Coasters International.

Thunderhead required 700,000 feet of Southern Yellow pine to construct the 3,230 foot long coaster.

It was the first roller coaster to feature a station fly through.

The ride has turns banked to 78 degrees, which was the steepest Great Coasters International had yet done when the ride opened.

12 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 24 riders per train.
